skill.name: shape
skill.description: Plan the UX and UI for a feature before writing code. Runs a structured discovery interview, then produces a design brief that guides implementation. Use during the planning phase to establish design direction, constraints, and strategy before any code is written.
skill.summary: Run a discovery interview and create a UX design brief before implementation.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography findings are false positives; SKILL.md contains markdown guidance and design-planning text, not cryptographic code. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 72 is also a false positive because the backticks only format `.impeccable.md` as inline markdown. Low residual risk remains because the skill asks the assistant to reference a local design context file.

low_findings.title: Local Design Context Reference
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to reference `.impeccable.md` for project design context. This is a legitimate design-planning behavior, but it may cause local project information to be read during use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reference is explicit and limited to design context. I found no evidence of network transfer, shell execution, credential access, or malicious handling of the referenced content.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography and Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed file contains no cryptographic implementation and no executable shell command. The line 72 backticks are markdown formatting around a filename.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the flagged line is prose with inline markdown. The broader weak cryptography alerts align with markdown and design text, not code that could perform cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Teams often start interface work before they understand the user goal, states, and constraints. This skill runs a structured discovery process and produces a design brief for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides a discovery interview before design decisions are made.
Identifies user goals, user state, content ranges, and edge cases.
Documents design direction, layout strategy, and interaction model.
Lists required feature states such as empty, loading, error, and success.
Creates a brief that can guide later implementation work.
Requests explicit user confirmation before the brief is complete.
limitations: It does not write production code or modify application files.
It depends on user answers when product context is missing.
It references Impeccable design context when available.
It provides planning guidance, not visual mockups or prototypes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Define the user goal, layout strategy, states, and interaction model before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Clarify a Complex Interface
use_cases.description: Turn unclear requirements into a structured design brief that engineers can follow.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Build Prompts
use_cases.description: Create a precise planning artifact before asking Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to build the UI.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small interface change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use shape to plan a settings page for notification preferences. Ask me the discovery questions first, then produce a design brief.

prompt_templates.title: Shape a Data-Heavy View
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a feature with many states.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use shape to plan a dashboard for support ticket trends. Include empty, loading, error, and high-volume data states.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Implementation Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning before another assistant builds the UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use shape to create a design brief for an onboarding checklist, then make it ready for implementation in Claude Code.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Ambiguous Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced discovery when product direction is unclear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use shape to interview me about a collaborative review workflow. Identify open questions, anti-goals, and the safest design direction.

output_examples.input: Plan a team invite flow for a SaaS admin console.
output_examples.output: A design brief covering the admin user, primary invite action, layout hierarchy, invite states, validation feedback, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Shape a mobile-first empty state for a finance app.
output_examples.output: A concise plan describing the user mindset, useful empty-state content, next best action, responsive constraints, and implementation references.

output_examples.input: Prepare a brief for a bulk edit table.
output_examples.output: A structured handoff with content ranges, selection behavior, confirmation flow, error handling, and accessibility considerations.

best_practices: Answer discovery questions with realistic data ranges and edge cases.
Share existing product patterns before asking for the final brief.
Confirm or correct the brief before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a quick code patch.
Do not skip discovery questions for complex user workflows.
Do not treat the brief as a substitute for user testing.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It creates a UX and UI design brief before code is written.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The brief can guide implementation work in Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

faq.question: What does the discovery interview cover?
faq.answer: It covers purpose, users, content, states, design goals, constraints, and anti-goals.

faq.question: Does it require an existing design system?
faq.answer: No. It can ask for context, but existing patterns improve the brief.

faq.question: What is the main output?
faq.answer: The output is a structured design brief with direction, layout, states, interactions, and open questions.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use care with sensitive design context because the skill may reference local project design notes.
